name: OpenAI Assistant for Hubspot Chat
description: Automated workflow: OpenAI Assistant for Hubspot Chat. This workflow integrates 10 different services: webhook, stickyNote, httpRequest, wait, airtable. It contains 59 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
shortDescription: OpenAI Assistant for Hubspot Chat workflow containing 34 nodes.

nodes.name: Sticky Note1

parameters.content:
Watch for new message on the chatbot. 
This can be triggered with [n8n chat widget]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} hubspot or other chat services.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note

parameters.content: Post assistant Message back to chat service, in this case Hubspot

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

parameters.content:
Search for Thread ID in a database. 

This database is maintaing references between messaging service thread id and OpenI Thread ID.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note7

parameters.content: POST a new message to existing thread.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note8

parameters.content:
Get Run Status:
If still in progress, run again. 
If action needed go to respective action.
If Completed, post message.

nodes.notes: This stickyNote node performs automated tasks as part of the workflow.

nodes.name: Sticky Note9

parameters.content:
Run required actions based on Assistant answer and respond to Assistant with the function answer. 

Each route is a function that you need to define inside your assistant configuration.

originalWorkflowJson.description: Automated workflow: OpenAI Assistant for Hubspot Chat. This workflow integrates 10 different services: webhook, stickyNote, httpRequest, wait, airtable. It contains 59 nodes and follows best practices for error handling and security.
originalWorkflowJson.notes: Excellent quality workflow: OpenAI Assistant for Hubspot Chat. This workflow has been optimized for production use with comprehensive error handling, security, and documentation.

parameters.content: Post assistant Message back to chat service, in this case Hubspot

Watch for new message on the chatbot. 
This can be triggered with [n8n chat widget]({{ $env.WEBHOOK_URL }} hubspot or other chat services.
Create a new Thread, save it to database and RUN

Search for Thread ID in a database. 

This database is maintaing references between messaging service thread id and OpenI Thread ID.
POST a new message to existing thread.

Get Run Status:
If still in progress, run again. 
If action needed go to respective action.
If Completed, post message.

Run required actions based on Assistant answer and respond to Assistant with the function answer. 

Each route is a function that you need to define inside your assistant configuration.

prerequisites: An active n8n instance installed locally, in a Docker container, or hosted on n8n Cloud.
Basic familiarity with importing JSON templates in n8n.
setupInstructions: Download the JSON configuration for this workflow.
Ensure you set up credentials for all connected integrations.
Test your trigger and run execution.
requiredCredentials: hubspot  O Auth2  A P I details
hubspot Developer  A P I details
open Ai  A P I details
airtable Token  A P I details
configurationSteps: Locate the webhook node named "Webhook".
Copy the Test Webhook URL provided by n8n.
Configure your source application (e.g. Stripe, Typeform) to send event payloads to this URL.
Open the "getHubspotMessage" node (n8n-nodes-base.httpRequest) and configure the action parameters, mappings, and fields.

No global variables node (Set node) is present. If you need dynamic constants, add a Set node after the trigger.
testingSteps: Click the "Test step" or "Listen for test event" button on your Trigger node.
Send a test trigger from your source service (e.g. fill a test form or trigger a webhook trigger).
Verify that the node canvas lights up green, indicating successful data flow.
Open the final destination node to confirm that output records were saved correctly.
instructions.expectedResult: Upon trigger activation, inputs will flow through the configured nodes and execute actions successfully.
troubleshooting: Check credential warning symbols. Most workflow issues stem from expired OAuth connections or missing API permissions.
Verify that the data keys coming from your Trigger node match the exact names mapped in subsequent nodes (e.g., {{ $json.body.email }}).
Open the Execution history tab in n8n to view logs and determine which node failed.
securityNotes: Never hardcode API keys, passwords, or authentication bearer tokens in the plain text fields of node parameters.
If you download or share this workflow JSON, sanitize any sensitive parameters or configuration nodes.
customizationIdeas: Add an email node at the end to notify you of successful executions.
Incorporate an AI LLM node to summarize trigger payloads before writing them to integrations.
